value.displayName: My New Load Test Run from PyTest

errorDetails.message: Data file 'testdata.csv' required by the test script is not found in the uploaded artifacts. Try again after uploading the file.

Description: Test run failed as 'testdata.csv' is missing or unreadable, causing repeated script failures during execution.
Resolution: Upload a valid and readable 'testdata.csv' file with test artifacts, then rerun the test.

errorDetails.message: No requests were sent from one or more engines during the test. Check your script and try again.

Description: No requests were sent because the test script failed initialization due to missing or unreadable 'testdata.csv'.
Resolution: Include the required data file and verify its accessibility in the script before running the load test.

errorDetails.message: The test was stopped due to a high error rate. Check your script and try again. In case the issue persists, raise a ticket with support. Learn more: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/load-testing/how-to-define-test-criteria#auto-stop-configuration

Description: The test run execution stopped as the error percentage exceeded 90.0 for 60.0 seconds.
Resolution: If high error rate during the test is a desired behavior, you can disable auto-stop or configure the thresholds. [Learn more](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/load-testing/how-to-define-test-criteria#auto-stop-configuration)

errorDetails.message: Test run exceeded the maximum allowed duration. Increase the maximum test run duration quota or limit the test run duration, and try again.

Description: The test run could not be executed as configured since the run duration exceeded the maximum allowed duration.
Resolution: Raise a support ticket to increase the quota for test duration. [Learn more](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/load-testing/resource-limits-quotas-capacity#request-quota-increases)
Alternatively, you can limit the test run duration, and try again.

errorDetails.message: The test was stopped due to a high error rate. Check your script and try again. In case the issue persists, raise a ticket with support. Learn more: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/load-testing/how-to-define-test-criteria#auto-stop-configuration

Description: The test run execution stopped as the error percentage exceeded 90.0 for 60.0 seconds.
Resolution: If high error rate during the test is a desired behavior, you can disable auto-stop or configure the thresholds. [Learn more](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/load-testing/how-to-define-test-criteria#auto-stop-configuration)

errorDetails.message: No requests were sent from one or more engines during the test. Check your script and try again.

Description: The test run failed because the base host was not specified in the test script, so no requests were sent from the engine.
Resolution: Set the 'host' attribute in the User class or use the '--host' option to specify the target server for load testing.

errorDetails.message: Host not specified for the load test. Specify the target host name in one of the following locations: User class, LOCUST_HOST environment variable, or the configuration file.

Description: Test engines could not be configured as Host is not specified for the load test.
Resolution: Specify the target host name in one of the following locations: User class, LOCUST_HOST environment variable, or the configuration file.

errorDetails.message: No requests were sent from one or more engines during the test. Check your script and try again.

Description: The test run execution failed as no requests were sent from one or more engines during the test.

errorDetails.message: The test run was created with request data not set to NONE, but no request data was generated during the test. In case the issue persists, raise a support ticket with the test run ID.

Description: The test run was created with request data not set to NONE, but no request data was generated during the test.
Resolution: Check your test script to ensure that request data is generated. Alternatively, set request data to NONE.

errorDetails.message: The test was stopped due to a high error rate. Check your script and try again. In case the issue persists, raise a ticket with support. Learn more: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/load-testing/how-to-define-test-criteria#auto-stop-configuration

errorDetails.message: The test was stopped due to maximum users per engine (250) exceeding the limit of 5. Try updating the test to reduce the maximum users per engine while increasing the number of engines to achieve target load, or increase the maximum users per engine in the auto stop criteria. Learn more: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/load-testing/how-to-define-test-criteria#auto-stop-configuration

Description: The test run execution stopped as the maximum concurrent users per engine (250) exceeded the limit of 5.
Resolution: Try updating the test to reduce the maximum users per engine while increasing the number of engines to achieve target load. [Learn more](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/load-testing/how-to-high-scale-load?tabs=portal#configure-load-parameters-for-a-load-test)

Alternatively, if you intend to have high number of users per engine, increase the maximum users per engine threshold in the auto stop criteria. [Learn more](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/load-testing/how-to-define-test-criteria#auto-stop-configuration)
